title: Retail health pulse
finding: The preserved ledger contains 541,909 lines and 25,900 invoices, producing gross revenue of £10,642,111 and net revenue of £9,748,131 after £893,980 in returns.
business_action: Track gross, returns, net revenue, AOV, customers, and order volume together; growth that raises returns is not healthy growth.

title: Market concentration
finding: United Kingdom is the largest market in the current data with £9,001,744 gross revenue.
business_action: Protect the core market while building an international growth shortlist using both revenue scale and return quality.

title: Seasonality and momentum
finding: The strongest observed month is 2023-11 at £1,503,867 revenue.
business_action: Bring inventory, staffing, and campaign launches forward by one replenishment lead time before the peak.

title: Weekday demand
finding: Friday generates the highest gross revenue in this transaction history.
business_action: Schedule email, paid media, and warehouse labor around the observed high-response days.

title: Trading-hour intensity
finding: The peak purchase hour is 10:00 with £1,444,815 revenue.
business_action: Concentrate flash offers and live support coverage around the highest-intent hours.

title: Product winners
finding: REGENCY CAKESTAND 3 TIER leads the revenue ranking at £174,157.
business_action: Keep winners in stock, feature them in acquisition creative, and use them as anchors for cross-sell bundles.

title: Product portfolio roles
finding: The velocity-value matrix separates traffic drivers, premium earners, scalable stars, and low-priority long-tail items.
business_action: Apply different inventory and merchandising rules by quadrant instead of one blanket reorder policy.

title: Product return exposure
finding: PAPER CRAFT , LITTLE BIRDIE has the largest material return exposure among adequately supported products.
business_action: Audit listing accuracy, packaging, supplier quality, and customer expectations for high-value/high-rate return items.

title: Market return exposure
finding: United Kingdom is the most material market-level return watchpoint in the ranked view.
business_action: Review delivery promises, carrier performance, duties, and localisation for high-return markets.

title: Basket economics
finding: Basket distribution reveals typical orders, large commercial baskets, and the tail that can distort averages.
business_action: Use median basket value for everyday targets and separate approval/service rules for extreme baskets.

title: Customer revenue concentration
finding: The top 20% of identified customers contribute 74.8% of identified-customer revenue.
business_action: Create a tiered retention programme, but avoid concentrating service only on current elites; nurture the next-value band too.

title: New versus repeat growth
finding: Monthly new-versus-repeat revenue separates acquisition-led spikes from durable customer relationships.
business_action: Judge campaigns on second-purchase conversion and repeat revenue, not only first-order sales.

title: Segment portfolio
finding: Champions is the largest revenue segment, contributing 64.9% of identified-customer value.
business_action: Assign a distinct CRM treatment, offer depth, and contact cadence to every segment.

title: Segment behaviour
finding: Segment profiles compare recency, frequency, monetary value, AOV, and cadence rather than relying on a cluster number.
business_action: Use profile differences to set measurable segment-specific goals and guardrails.

title: At-risk recovery
finding: At-risk customers represent £557,121 in historical customer value.
business_action: Prioritise win-back by value at risk and product affinity; use a stepped incentive instead of an immediate blanket discount.

title: Purchase cadence
finding: Customer cadence shows when a next purchase becomes late relative to the shopper's own pattern.
business_action: Trigger reminders from expected cadence, not a single fixed inactivity threshold for everyone.

title: Cohort retention
finding: Cohort curves expose whether later customer vintages are becoming more or less loyal after acquisition.
business_action: Compare retention by acquisition month and investigate offer, channel, or product-mix changes behind deterioration.

title: Price-band productivity
finding: Price-band mix shows whether growth is driven by unit volume, premium value, or zero-price activity.
business_action: Balance conversion-friendly value products with premium attach opportunities and protect margin by band.

title: Zero-price governance
finding: There are 2,515 zero-price lines in the preserved ledger.
business_action: Tag authorised samples/promotions and investigate all remaining zero-price activity as revenue leakage or master-data risk.

title: Data-quality exposure
finding: Missing CustomerID is the most prevalent quality flag in the summary.
business_action: Treat data quality as a commercial KPI: improve capture at source while retaining an auditable exception layer.

title: Product affinity
finding: Item-based collaborative filtering produces five customer-behaviour neighbours for recommendation-ready products.
business_action: Use high-similarity pairs in product pages, basket add-ons, and post-purchase journeys; A/B test uplift before full rollout.
